title: Burial Stones
content:
The blue sky arches wide
    From hill to hill;
    The little grasses stand
    Upright and still.

    Only these stones to tell
    The deadly strife,
    The all-important schemes,
    The greed for life.

    For they are gone, who fought;
    But still the skies
    Stretch blue, aloof, unchanged,
    From rise to rise.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Frank James Prewett, titled "Burial Stones", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
